questions.question: E-commerce platform: PostgreSQL (orders, customers) + clickstream + payment API. Нужен real-time order tracking + daily BI. Как организовать ingestion layer?

options.text: Batch ETL каждый час для всех источников

Kafka как central event bus: PostgreSQL → Debezium CDC → Kafka (real-time). Clickstream → SDK → Kafka producer → Kafka. Payment API → webhook receiver → Kafka. Topic per source. Consumers: batch (Bronze) + streaming (real-time tracking) читают из одного Kafka
Direct connection из каждого consumer к source
Files dump на S3 каждые 15 минут

questions.explanation: Kafka = decoupling layer. Sources publish once → N consumers read independently. CDC: real-time changes без нагрузки на OLTP. Clickstream: high volume, async write to Kafka. Payments: webhooks → producer. Unified bus → multiple consumers (Bronze lakehouse, real-time ClickHouse, feature store) из одних данных.

questions.question: Capstone architecture review: команда предлагает начать с полной реализации всех 3 serving layers (BI + ML + Real-time) одновременно. Ваша рекомендация?

options.text: Начать с ML pipeline — самое сложное

MVP first: начать с 1 source (PostgreSQL CDC) → Kafka → Bronze → Silver → Gold → 1 BI dashboard. Затем итерировать: добавить clickstream, затем ML features, затем real-time. Complexity kills projects — доставить value early, расширять incrementally
Отложить все serving layers на потом

questions.explanation: MVP approach: 1 source → full pipeline → 1 consumer = working end-to-end in weeks. All 3 serving layers simultaneously = months of work before any value. Iterative: MVP → add sources → add serving layers. Each iteration delivers value. Risk: full build = integration hell, delayed feedback, potential pivot wasted.
